title: 5. Lab - Debugging a Failing Tool using Trace Analysis

blocks.content: In this lab, you will run an agent that fails when interacting with a local inventory database. You will use `adk-web` trace diagnostics to identify the parameter format bug, correct the source code, and verify execution success.
Trigger the agent to retrieve stock information from a database file. The query fails silently or returns an error response:

blocks.code: adk run --prompt "Retrieve stock details for item id 8802 and export schema report."

blocks.content: Open `http://localhost:8501` in your browser. Inspect the last run history pane. Under 'Tool Failures', locate the active error logs:
Step 3: Correct the Tool Schema Definition
The model passed `8802` as an integer, but the python code was expecting a string format. We will update the tool's python signature type hint to accept both `int` and `str` parameters:

blocks.code:
# Before:
# def fetch_stock_details(item_id: str)

# After (corrected type definition):
def fetch_stock_details(item_id: int | str):
    """
    Retrieves local inventory stock numbers.
    Args:
        item_id (int | str): The ID of the item.
    """
    # Code handles integer string conversion safely
    sanitized_id = str(item_id)
    # Run database query...

blocks.content: Execute the agent once more. Review the transaction history in adk-web to confirm a green success tag is displayed next to the tool invocation step.
